rationale.summary: Vote: ABSTAIN. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (enacted), evidence quality was insufficient for a reliable YES or NO decision. Decisive reason: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "Amaru is an open-source project implementing a new fully interoperable block-producing node for Cardano." though 8 review blocker(s) remain open. What would enable a directional vote: Provide: treasury_amount_lovelace is missing for a treasury withdrawal; Provide treasury_amount_lovelace is missing for a treasury withdrawal. sections.title: Intake
missing: treasury_amount_lovelace is missing for a treasury withdrawal
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: Amaru is an open-source project implementing a new fully interoperable block-producing node for Cardano., It aims to improve the network's overall accessibility and robustness without compromising its safety and security., This treasury withdrawal of ₳1.5M follows a previously submitted and now approved budget proposal., It is split across five script addresses each covering a scope of a work (ledger, consensus, mercenaries and marketing) and a contingency reserve.
Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Amaru is an open-source project implementing a new fully interoperable block-producing node for Cardano.
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): It aims to improve the network's overall accessibility and robustness without compromising its safety and security.
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): This treasury withdrawal of ₳1.5M follows a previously submitted and now approved budget proposal.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): It is split across five script addresses each covering a scope of a work (ledger, consensus, mercenaries and marketing) and a contingency reserve.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Who we are Amaru is a multi-entity effort that currently sits under PRAGMA: a member-based, not-for-profit association for open-source blockchain software projects.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Five members with equal power currently drive PRAGMA's strategy and operations: Blink Labs, Cardano Foundation, dcSpark, Sundae Labs, and TxPipe.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unsustainable (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Amaru is an open-source project implementing a new fully interoperable block-producing node for Cardano." though 8 review blocker(s) remain open.
Strongest NO: an unresolved blocker (treasury_amount_lovelace is missing for a treasury withdrawal) means costs or weak controls may outweigh the claimed benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
missing: treasury_amount_lovelace is missing for a treasury withdrawal
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
